vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear
Motor pod / pusher prop mount dummy with bolt pattern and cooling path (non-operational)
Equipment bay door, cooling inlet, motor mount, bolt circle, fastener pattern, harness path, wing spar reinforcement

risk.desc: Potential for salt-air corrosion affecting fasteners and electronics
risk.mit: Use corrosion-resistant fasteners and conformal coating on electronics

risk.desc: High-wind launch may cause structural stress beyond design limits
risk.mit: Conduct wind tunnel tests to validate structural integrity under high-wind conditions

risk.desc: Service access panel may obstruct equipment bay door operation
risk.mit: Ensure service access panel design allows for full door operation without obstruction

risk.desc: Cooling inlet may become blocked by debris during flight
risk.mit: Design cooling inlet with debris filter and regular maintenance access

risk.desc: Insufficient thermal management may lead to component failure
risk.mit: Implement additional thermal management features such as heat sinks or active cooling

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

physics_paths.description: Wing spar reinforcement transfers bending loads to pod structure
Cooling air from motor pylon routed to cooling inlet
Heat from electronics transferred to pod structure for dissipation
Equipment bay door motion constrained by pod structure
Electrical harness from motor pylon to electronics bay

integration_opportunities.description: Equipment bay door provides access for maintenance and module replacement
Cooling inlet allows airflow for thermal management of electronics
Wing spar reinforcement integrates with wing structure for load transfer

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for surface quality and dimensional accuracy

verification_focus.description: Verify mass of pod structure does not exceed 500g
Verify spar reinforcement can handle 3g wing bending load
Verify electrical harness path is free of interference

risk.desc: Potential corrosion from salt-air exposure.
risk.mit: Apply corrosion-resistant coating and use salt-air resistant materials.

risk.desc: High wind launch may cause structural stress.
risk.mit: Design spar with additional reinforcement and perform wind tunnel testing.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion may affect alignment.
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion joints and conduct thermal cycling tests.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance in moving joints.
risk.mit: Increase clearance in critical interfaces and conduct fit checks.

risk.desc: Potential for load path discontinuity.
risk.mit: Ensure continuous load path from spar to fuselage frames with fittings at every load transfer.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path
physics_paths: Load path: lift force from wing skins to spar, through carry-through to fuselage frames
Thermal path: heat from sun through spar to fuselage, cooling through airflow
Service access: battery bay access via slide rail and CG marks
Motion: wing rotation at center joint
Pressure: air pressure on wing skins transferred to spar
integration_opportunities: Battery bay access via slide rail

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.support: Build orientation optimized for minimal support structure
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit, CMM for critical dimensions

verification_focus: Load path continuity from spar to fuselage frames
Thermal management through spar and fuselage
Service access for battery and payload bay
Anti-rotation key functionality at center joint

risk.desc: Potential salt-air corrosion due to offshore wind farm inspection mission.
risk.mit: Use salt-resistant materials and coatings.

risk.desc: High wind launch capability may strain wing rib structure.
risk.mit: Increase spar thickness and rib stiffness.

risk.desc: Limited service access for battery bay and payload bay.
risk.mit: Design larger access panels for easier maintenance.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to wide temperature range.
risk.mit: Use materials with low thermal expansion coefficients.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under extreme loading conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct FEA analysis to validate structural integrity.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing planform, airfoil section evidence, winglets, elevon/control surface cutouts with hinges

generated_parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
generated_parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

verify.rule: All required features (servo_pocket, control_horn, pushrod_channel, control_surface_cutout, control_horn_mount, removable_cover, wing_bolt_pattern, power_connector, access_hatch) are present in part_tree and geometry_ops.
Mass estimation is within the acceptable range for the subsystem.
P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
Adjacent interfaces or mounting datums are named and connection_type, DOF, clearance_mm, and required_feature are specified.
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.
Boundary conditions are defined for downstream CFD/FEA.

risk.desc: The removable cover may not provide sufficient protection against salt-air corrosion.
risk.mit: Apply a corrosion-resistant coating or use a more durable material for the removable cover.

risk.desc: The control surface cutout may be too small, potentially limiting the range of motion.
risk.mit: Review the design and adjust the dimensions of the control surface cutout to ensure adequate travel range.

risk.desc: The power connector may not be easily accessible for field service.
risk.mit: Ensure that the power connector is located in a more accessible area or provide a clear access hatch.

risk.desc: The wing bolt pattern may not be robust enough to withstand the launch loads.
risk.mit: Review the bolt pattern design and increase the strength if necessary.

risk.desc: The control horn mount may not be adequately secured, leading to potential failure under high wind conditions.
risk.mit: Add additional fasteners or use a locking mechanism to secure the control horn mount.

risk.desc: The motor mount may not be precisely aligned with the thrust line due to manufacturing tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement a precision alignment tool during assembly.

risk.desc: The cooling inlet may not maintain airflow at 15g launch due to design constraints.
risk.mit: Conduct airflow analysis to optimize cooling inlet design.

risk.desc: The fastener pattern may not withstand 8Nm torque due to material limitations.
risk.mit: Select a material that can withstand the required torque.

risk.desc: The electrical harness may be routed with strain due to tight routing paths.
risk.mit: Optimize routing paths to ensure strain-free harness installation.

risk.desc: The motor pod may not be structurally sound at 3g wing bending due to insufficient reinforcement.
risk.mit: Add additional stiffeners or reinforce the motor pod structure.

risk.desc: The payload bay may drift into weaponized or operational payload details.
risk.mit: Regularly review design to ensure it remains non-weaponized and display-only.

risk.desc: Battery is not independently removable, which may block CG adjustment.
risk.mit: Design the battery bay to use slide rails and independent removal mechanisms.

risk.desc: Structural members may end at skins or bays without fitting, breaking load path.
risk.mit: Ensure continuous structural members with fittings at every load transfer point.

risk.desc: Interfaces may lack tolerance or fit specification, leading to assembly issues.

risk.desc: Dimensions may be unrealistic or placeholder values, affecting manufacturability.
risk.mit: Ensure all dimensions are physically plausible and match the subsystem scale.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: payload - role: Non-weapon camera/gimbal sensor pod bay with removable insert and window

physics_paths.path: Structural loads from wing bending and servo actuation are transferred through the spar to the camera bay.
Airflow around the wing passes over the optical window, providing cooling.
Thermal dissipation path from camera electronics to the wing structure through the mounting interface.
Camera gimbal motion is constrained by the gimbal mount and actuated by servos.
External pressure is resisted by the structural integrity of the wing and camera bay.
Electrical power and data signals are routed from the wing's avionics bay to the camera bay via harnesses and connectors.

integration_opportunities.path: Ensure continuous load path from wing spar to sensor bay.
Maintain thermal dissipation path from camera electronics to wing structure.
Route electrical harnesses from avionics bay to camera pod.

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: visual and tactile inspection of spar_socket and anti-rotation keys

risk.desc: Potential thermal hotspots near battery pack may exceed safe operating temperatures.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management features such as heat sinks or active cooling.

risk.desc: High wind conditions may cause structural deformation, affecting payload integration.
risk.mit: Increase structural rigidity and perform wind tunnel testing.

risk.desc: Salt-air corrosion may degrade electrical connections over time.
risk.mit: Use corrosion-resistant materials and coatings for all electrical components.

risk.desc: Incorrect assembly sequence may lead to misalignment and structural failure.
risk.mit: Ensure clear assembly instructions and training for assembly personnel.

risk.desc: Insufficient strain relief on cables may cause damage during operation.
risk.mit: Implement strain relief features at all cable exits and ensure proper routing.
